draft.title: a CRM for freelancers
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a CRM for freelancers

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers respond better to interfaces that feel personal and inviting rather than corporate and cold
facade.label: Warm glow
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers softer, ambient lighting; branded/human touches; less sterile dashboard; possibly cream/amber/rust tones over grays/blues
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers clean, minimal, professional aesthetic; cool tones; high contrast; corporate feel acceptable or preferred

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer snappy, immediate feedback on client actions rather than animated transitions that delay clarity
facade.axisTargeted: Snappy instant feedback vs fluid animated transitions
facade.label: Instant updates
facade.acceptImplies: User values speed and directness in CRM interactions; prefers updates to appear immediately without motion
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer motion/animation to guide attention and create intentional pacing; values elegance over raw speed

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer viewing clients as individual cards over dense table rows
facade.label: Client list as cards
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should use card-based layouts for contact/project summaries; prioritize scannable visual blocks over spreadsheet-style data display
facade.rejectImplies: Table or list-based layouts are preferred; users want data density and comparison across rows; cards may feel inefficient for managing multiple clients

evidence.hypothesis: Freelancers respond better to interfaces that feel personal and inviting rather than corporate and cold
evidence.implication: User prefers softer, ambient lighting; branded/human touches; less sterile dashboard; possibly cream/amber/rust tones over grays/blues

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer navigation + client overview in one glance, trusting density over whitespace
facade.axisTargeted: Layout: sidebar vs full-width; information density: compact vs spacious
facade.label: Sidebar + compact list
facade.acceptImplies: User values quick scanning of multiple clients/projects; expects vertical navigation; comfortable with smaller type and tight spacing
facade.rejectImplies: User wants breathing room, prefers tabs or top nav, finds dense layouts overwhelming or dated

axes.label: Whether they prefer minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a distraction-free workspace or one that feels more dynamic and branded
Whether they favor sidebar/list-based navigation vs. card/grid layouts—reveals how they want to organize and access client/project information at a glance
Whether they respond to professional/formal tone vs. casual/encouraging tone—reveals if they want a business tool that feels corporate or one that feels like a supportive partner
Whether they prefer calm, spacious layouts vs. information-dense, action-oriented layouts—reveals their comfort with visual complexity and urgency cues
Whether they want data presented as simple numbers/status vs. rich context with charts/timelines—reveals how much analytical depth they need to manage their freelance business
Whether they expect instant feedback and snappy interactions vs. smooth, deliberate transitions—reveals if they value speed and directness or prefer a more thoughtful, guided experience

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they prefer minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a distraction-free workspace or one that feels more dynamic and branded
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they favor sidebar/list-based navigation vs. card/grid layouts—reveals how they want to organize and access client/project information at a glance
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they respond to professional/formal tone vs. casual/encouraging tone—reveals if they want a business tool that feels corporate or one that feels like a supportive partner
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they prefer calm, spacious layouts vs. information-dense, action-oriented layouts—reveals their comfort with visual complexity and urgency cues
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they want data presented as simple numbers/status vs. rich context with charts/timelines—reveals how much analytical depth they need to manage their freelance business
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they expect instant feedback and snappy interactions vs. smooth, deliberate transitions—reveals if they value speed and directness or prefer a more thoughtful, guided experience
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer high contrast layouts that separate navigation from active workspace
facade.label: Dark sidebar, light content
facade.acceptImplies: User leans toward clear visual hierarchy and reduced cognitive load in navigation; likely appreciates professional, minimal aesthetics
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer more unified color schemes, softer contrast, or equal visual weight across regions; possibly drawn to modern flat or colorful interfaces

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers feel overwhelmed by cluttered dashboards and prefer stripped-down interfaces that show only essential metrics and actions
facade.axisTargeted: organic vs digital / calm vs energetic
facade.label: Dashboard minimalism
facade.acceptImplies: Design direction: sparse layouts, hidden menus, progressive disclosure of features; prioritize invoice status and upcoming deadlines only; reduce visual noise
facade.rejectImplies: Design direction: richer information density; more visible at-a-glance stats; dashboard should feel comprehensive and powerful, not austere; embrace ambient data visibility

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer a CRM that feels like a helpful buddy rather than enterprise software—using conversational language, friendly tone, and approachable UI.
facade.label: Casual and chatty
facade.acceptImplies: Design should use contractions, warm copy, friendly icons, lighter colors, encouraging microcopy. Reject corporate jargon and stiff layouts.
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer polished, professional, minimal tone—clean typography, formal language, corporate aesthetic. Signals either personality preference or market positioning concern.
